findings.evidence: Basic.lean:110-188 hosts the IsCompletelyMonotone namespace (add, smul, nonneg, antitoneOn, etc.); the congr lemma is the same kind of general API but lives in Bernstein.lean.
findings.fix: Move IsCompletelyMonotone.congr next to the other IsCompletelyMonotone API. It only needs IsCompletelyMonotone + iteratedDerivWithin_congr (already imported by Closure.lean), so Closure.lean is a clean home, or Basic.lean with the IteratedDeriv.Lemmas import added.
findings.issue: IsCompletelyMonotone.congr is general API for the IsCompletelyMonotone predicate defined in Basic.lean — it states CM depends only on values on [0,∞) and has nothing Bernstein-specific. Placing it here forces any consumer wanting CM congruence to import the Bernstein file.

findings.evidence: Diff lines 44-93 define the predicate and its entire API with no dependency on IsBernsteinFunction; grep shows no other use of IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi outside Bernstein.lean, so the only access path to this CM-on-Ioi theory is importing the Bernstein file.
findings.fix: Move IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi and its namespace to the CompletelyMonotone Basic/Closure files (or a dedicated CompletelyMonotone/Ioi-style file), leaving Bernstein.lean to contain only the Bernstein predicate and its API.
findings.issue: IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi is a standalone general predicate plus a full namespace API (contDiffOn, nonneg, deriv_nonpos, congr, add, smul, and the IsCompletelyMonotone→Ioi bridge) about complete monotonicity on (0,∞). It is generic CM theory, not Bernstein-specific, and its only home is this downstream file.

summary: The Bernstein.lean file is correctly placed under CompletelyMonotone/ with specific, directly-used imports, but it buries general IsCompletelyMonotone API that belongs in the existing earlier files (Basic.lean/Closure.lean).
